successCriteria.description: File src/shared/types/leave.types.ts exists and exports a LeaveType enum with exactly three members: ANNUAL, SICK, EMERGENCY
File src/shared/types/leave.types.ts exists and exports a LeaveStatus enum with exactly four members: PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED
File tests/unit/shared/types/leave.types.test.ts exists with Jest tests that verify all LeaveType enum values (ANNUAL, SICK, EMERGENCY) are defined
File tests/unit/shared/types/leave.types.test.ts exists with Jest tests that verify all LeaveStatus enum values (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED) are defined
The TypeScript source compiles without errors (tsc --noEmit passes) and Jest tests pass
No files other than src/shared/types/leave.types.ts and tests/unit/shared/types/leave.types.test.ts are created or modified

constraints: LeaveType enum must contain exactly ANNUAL, SICK, EMERGENCY — no other values
LeaveStatus enum must contain exactly PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED — no other values
The file must use TypeScript string enums (not numeric enums, not const objects, not union types) to serve as both a type and a runtime value
No other files are created or modified in this phase — this is a single-file type definition plus its unit test
The test file must use Jest (the declared test framework in package.json) and import from the source file using relative paths
No use of `any` type — strict TypeScript with no implicit any and strict null checks per tsconfig.json
outOfScope: Phase 2 — Employee module (model, repository, service): deferred to a later phase
Phase 3 — Policy module (model, repository, service): deferred to a later phase
Phase 4 — Balance module (model, repository, service): deferred to a later phase
Phase 5 — Notification module (model, repository, service): deferred to a later phase
Phase 6 — Leave module model and repository: deferred to a later phase
Phase 7 — Leave module service with workflow orchestration: deferred to a later phase
Phase 8 — Zod validation schemas: deferred to a later phase
Phase 9 — Leave controller: deferred to a later phase
Phase 10 — Fastify routes and app registration: deferred to a later phase
Any database schema, repository, service, controller, route, or validation logic — this phase is purely type definitions

ambiguities.description: ARCHITECTURE.md declares "Test framework: Vitest" but HARNESS.json declares "testFramework": "Jest" and package.json uses Jest. The intent says "Jest unit test". Which framework is authoritative? (HARNESS.json takes precedence per architecture-agent rules, so Jest is correct.)

ARCHITECTURE.md references src/shared/types/leave.types.ts as containing LeaveType, LeaveStatus, NotificationType, AuditAction, EntityType — but the intent only specifies LeaveType and LeaveStatus for this phase. Should the file include only the two enums specified in the intent, or also the additional types mentioned in ARCHITECTURE.md?
options: Only LeaveType and LeaveStatus (as specified in the intent)
All five types (as referenced in ARCHITECTURE.md)
LeaveType and LeaveStatus now; others added in later phases

ambiguities.description: The intent says "enum" but does not specify whether to use TypeScript regular enum or const enum. Regular enum generates reverse-mapped runtime objects; const enum is inlined at compile time and produces no runtime object, which would make the Jest test unable to iterate enum values.
options: Regular string enum (generates runtime object, testable)
const enum (inlined, no runtime object — tests would need a different approach)
